audit_methods: manual review of validator execution paths

findings.description: artifact_packager.py stub builders (_stub_docx_marker, _stub_recommendations, _stub_execution_metadata) emitted stub files without any governed fallback record — gap was auditable only by humans.
findings.resolution: Fixed in Step 1 (PR #1174): each stub builder now calls _emit_fallback_artifact() before returning the stub payload. package_artifacts() returns fallback_artifacts in its result dict.

findings.description: _stub_not_implemented in validator_engine.py returns status='blocked' and blocking=True — already fail-closed. No silent pass.
findings.resolution: No fix required. Tests added in tests/test_validator_engine_no_stubs.py to lock this behavior.

findings.description: Multiple except Exception: pass patterns in non-validator modules (governance/, dashboard/, learning/, etc.). While not on validator paths, these suppress exceptions silently.
findings.resolution: Scope: governance/non-validator modules only. These are not on the critical validator execution path. Logged as should_fix for future hardening iteration.

findings.description: return True patterns in predicate/guard functions (not validators). Functions like is_valid(), can_proceed(), transition_allowed() — Boolean predicates, not artifact-emitting validators.
findings.resolution: No fix required. These are correct Boolean predicate patterns, not governance validators.

summary.notes: The critical validator path (validator_engine.py) is fail-closed. No must-fix issues remain after Step 1 fixes.

next_actions: RT1-003 exception swallowing: schedule for future hardening sprint (non-blocking)
Continue to Step 4 (SLO/SLI) and Step 5 (enforcement closure)
